name: Glendale
description_short: Glendale Federal Credit Union is a member-owned financial cooperative established in 1951, serving over 8,100 members with $102.3M in assets from its Glendale, California headquarters.
description_long: Glendale Federal Credit Union was established in 1951 and is headquartered in Glendale, California. As a credit union, it operates as a member-owned cooperative rather than a for-profit institution. The organization has grown to serve over 8,100 loyal members and stewards approximately $102.3 million in assets, indicating stable mid-sized operations within the credit union sector. Glendale FCU offers a comprehensive suite of consumer banking and lending products. Members can access checking accounts, savings accounts, money market accounts, certificates of deposit (CDs), and IRA/KEOGH accounts. The credit union provides lending products including auto loans (new and used vehicles), personal loans, credit cards, and mortgage options (fixed-rate mortgages up to 15+ years and adjustable-rate mortgages). Digital banking services include online banking, mobile app access for iOS and Android, bill pay, online loan payments, mobile payments, remote deposit capture, and e-statements. Additional services include wire transfers, financial calculators, and financial counseling and education programs. Glendale FCU distinguishes itself through its focus on specialized financial services for government sector employees, suggesting targeted membership eligibility criteria beyond general public access. The credit union maintains a lean operational footprint with just 1 physical branch and over 13 dedicated employees as of 2026, reflecting an efficiency-focused model. Member account distribution shows strong engagement: over 7,900 regular savings accounts, 3,500+ share draft accounts, 88+ money market accounts, and 300+ certificates of deposit, demonstrating diverse member utilization of available products. However, the credit union currently faces significant operational challenges that prospective members should consider carefully. The website indicates the lobby is currently closed and that Glendale "may be experiencing service issues." Recent member reviews are deeply concerning—the most recent review reports inability to access accounts online and by telephone, with the reviewer expressing being "very unhappy." The Main Office location has a poor member rating of 1.0/5 stars based on available reviews, and the website includes an outage reporting system for online banking, mobile app, website, phone, and ATM services, suggesting recurring technical problems.
services: Mortgage loans (fixed-rate 15+ years, fixed-rate 15 years or less, adjustable-rate)
Online banking with bill pay and external account transfers
pros: Established nonprofit credit union (since 1951) with over 8,100 members and $102.3M in assets
Comprehensive product suite including checking, savings, CDs, auto loans, personal loans, mortgages, and credit cards
Mobile banking app available for both iOS and Android with remote deposit capture capability
Member-focused services including financial counseling, financial education workshops, and community Q&A forums
Specialized membership options tailored for government sector employees
Competitive lending options with mortgage calculators and loan payment tools available
cons: Currently experiencing documented service outages affecting online banking, mobile app, telephone access, and ATM functionality
Physical branch accessibility severely limited with only 1 branch location and 0 ATMs
Recent member reviews indicate persistent account access problems both online and via telephone
Main Office location has extremely poor member rating of 1.0/5 stars based on available reviews
Website explicitly states lobby is 'currently closed' with ongoing service issue reports
best_for: Government sector employees who qualify for specialized membership eligibility
Members seeking a traditional credit union with comprehensive lending products who are not currently experiencing access issues
Borrowers interested in auto loans, mortgages, or personal loans from a nonprofit institution
diagnosis: Glendale Federal Credit Union is best suited for government sector employees who qualify for membership and seek comprehensive banking and lending services from a nonprofit credit union. However, prospective members must carefully weigh current severe service disruptions—including documented inability to access accounts online and by telephone, lobby closures, and a 1.0/5 star member rating—before joining. The credit union should be approached cautiously until service reliability improves substantially.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
